methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
CORE POWER is a Growth-stage climate and energy company founded by Mikal Bøe that has raised $100M+. The valuation assessment draws on a comparative set of 9 same-sector peers, including 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private firms such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the peer universe lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers to support a robust median-based approach; at least three tight peer matches would be required to apply that method reliably.

Consequently, the analysis relies on stage-progression methodology. Using the $100M total raised as a valuation proxy, application of a typical Growth-stage step-up multiplier of 1.2x yields an implied value of $120M. This represents the primary quantitative anchor.

The triangulation supports a range of $102M to $138M, with a median point of $120M. This narrow range reflects the single-method foundation rather than cross-validation across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of recent comparable transaction data at CORE POWER's specific stage and subsector focus within climate and energy, which limits confidence in the step-up multiplier itself. Additional clarity on revenue scale, capital efficiency, and technology differentiation relative to named peers would strengthen conviction in this valuation band.
